mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment rise, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of rising industrial production, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with rising policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect is dominated by a single proxy in stepwise regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that supports aggregate consumption and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth, or the signal produces negative returns during periods of monthly job additions exceeding 200 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real gross domestic product confirms an economic recession, which triggers widespread corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 50% of quarters following negative GDP growth, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a synchronized economic expansion that boosts equity risk appetite and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns when either series moves in the opposite direction, or the interaction term loses predictive significance in out-of-sample validation.
